     The Numbers Business Report

Quarantine (2008)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $31,691,811Details
International Box Office $10,232,963
Worldwide Box Office $41,924,774
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $15,227,661
Total $15,227,661
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Television reporter Angela Vidal and her cameraman are assigned to spend the night shift with a Los Angeles Fire Station. After a routine 911 call takes them to a small apartment building, they find police officers already on the scene in response to blood curdling screams coming from one of the apartment units. They soon learn that a woman living in the building has been infected by something unknown. After a few of the residents are viciously attacked, they try to escape with the news crew in tow, only to find that the CDC has quarantined the building. Phones, internet, televisions and cell phone access have been cut-off, and officials are not relaying information to those locked inside. When the quarantine is finally lifted, the only evidence of what took place is the news crew's videotape.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$14,211,321 (44.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.23 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:75.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$12,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,461 opening theaters/2,463 max. theaters, 3.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $52,790,256

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 10th, 2008 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
Video Release: February 17th, 2009 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for bloody violent and disturbing content, terror and language
Running Time: 89 minutes
Franchise: Quarantine
Cast, crew, or production detail: Foreign-Language Remake, Remake
Subgenre: Isolation Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Screen Gems, Vertigo Entertainment, Andale Pictures, Filmax Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Television reporter Angela Vidal and her cameraman are assigned to spend the night shift with a Los Angeles Fire Station. After a routine 911 call takes them to a small apartment building, they find police officers already on the scene in response to blood curdling screams coming from one of the apartment units. They soon learn that a woman living in the building has been infected by something unknown. After a few of the residents are viciously attacked, they try to escape with the news crew in tow, only to find that the CDC has quarantined the building. Phones, internet, televisions and cell phone access have been cut-off, and officials are not relaying information to those locked inside. When the quarantine is finally lifted, the only evidence of what took place is the news crew's videotape.
